source: plan/bearings.md § Article hero art — colorful keyboard SVGs
template: directive: simple line drawing + single splash of color illustrative of subject
subject: Upward-trending sparkline (8 data points) on a baseline with vertical tick guides and faint area-fill beneath; latest data point rendered as a focal cursor dot in bronze theme accent
prompt: Re-scale the sparkline geometry so the inked content dominates the canvas at every form factor. Baseline runs x=90→1110 (~85% width, ~7.5% padding each side). Peak data point at (1110, 110); baseline at y=670 (~75% height with ~15% top margin and ~10% bottom). Cursor outer ring extends a few pixels above the peak data point, which the brief explicitly permits. Keep the existing didactic moves intact: same ochre/brass splash hue, same warm-grey 2px stroke, same 8-point upward-trending shape, same focal cursor (filled dot + outer ring) on the rightmost point, same right-side annotation tick, same baseline + 8 vertical guide ticks, same low-fill area sweep beneath the line, same ochre wash band behind the chart. Rescaling, not redesigning.
revisionReason: User-jot 2026-05-09 (CRITIQUE.md commit 40fefe2): the previous geometry left the chart sitting in the lower third (sparkline filled ~67% width, ~28% height) which read small on mobile and underwhelming on desktop. This revision tightens padding so the chart fills the canvas — sparkline now ~85% width and ~75% height — while preserving every didactic element of the original ("i love the hero art ... can we nip the padding so the chart appears bigger across the form factors").
